name: cold_day_heat_all_day_with_override
description: Cold day (forecast high 48F). HVAC in heat mode at comfort_heat (70F). At 10:00, user bumps thermostat to 74F (override detected → confirmed after 10 min). At 22:30, bedtime event fires — but override is active, so bedtime setback is cleared by _handle_bedtime() which calls clear_manual_override() then applies setback normally.
notes: Tests cold day + override + bedtime interaction.
Key behavior: _handle_bedtime() in automation.py calls clear_manual_override() at the start, then applies setback.
This means bedtime does NOT skip setback when override is active — it clears the override first, then sets back.
The simulator's _handle_bedtime() matches this: sets manual_override_active=False, then computes setback.
Override detection/confirmation (override_detected, override_confirmed) require Gap 3 — marked track:integration.
Logic-track assertions: classification_applied (heat, 70F), no_action during classification while override active, setback_applied at bedtime (override cleared by bedtime handler first).
The no_action during classification at 14:30 requires manual_override_active=True at that point, which in the simulator requires Gap 3. Marked integration.
setback_applied at bedtime IS logic-track because simulator _handle_bedtime() clears override and applies setback.

events.note: Cold winter morning. Indoor 68F (below comfort_heat=70F), outdoor 30F.
Cold-day classification: hvac_mode=off (classifier says skip heating).
User bumps thermostat to heat mode — override detection fires
User bumps thermostat to 74F in heat mode. Override detection fires; confirm window starts (10 min).
10 min elapsed. Thermostat still at user-set 74F → override confirmed.
30-min classification cycle: classifier still says off; override should suppress this (no_action).
Bedtime event. _handle_bedtime() clears manual override first, then applies heat setback. Default depth = 4F below comfort_heat=70F → 66F (above setback_heat=62F floor).

assertions.reason: User bumped thermostat while classification says heat at 70F — override detection pending.
assertions.note: Requires Gap 3 (override confirmation delay) in simulate.py.

assertions.reason: 10 minutes elapsed with thermostat still user-controlled → override_confirm_seconds=600 expired → override confirmed.

Classification cycle while override active → no_action (classification stored but HVAC unchanged). In the simulator, this is only logic-track if override was actually confirmed — but since Gap 3 is not yet implemented, the simulator won't have manual_override_active=True here. Marking integration until Gap 3 is closed.
assertions.note: Requires Gap 3 to set manual_override_active=True after confirmation. Once Gap 3 is closed this becomes logic-track.

assertions.reason: Bedtime event while override active — bedtime setback skipped (mirrors production Issue #204).

verdict.summary: Cold day override suppresses classification cycles; bedtime clears override and applies setback normally
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (heat, 70F) → override_detected (10:00) → override_confirmed (10:10) → no_action on classification (14:30) → setback_applied (22:30, 66F)
verdict.expected_behavior: CA respects manual override during the day; bedtime event always clears override and applies setback regardless of override state
